title: pelargonium × affluens
intro: Pelargonium × affluens, also known as the rose pelargonium, is a perennial succulent plant in the family Geraniaceae, native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of geraniol and linalool. The health benefits of Pelargonium × affluens include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antiseptic properties, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Pelargonium × affluens can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and other health conditions.
